findings.19: All folded proteins show significant backbone dihedral synchronisation (Rayleigh p<0.01 for 4/5). Dihedrals ARE coupled phase oscillators.
findings.20_falsified: Contact decay alpha=0.003-0.075 does NOT match SCPN alpha=0.3. Different topology class — proteins have longer-range contacts.
findings.21: Villin headpiece (fastest folder) has highest R=0.57. Fastest folder = most synchronised.
findings.22: Larger proteins (ubiquitin, crambin) show GOE-like level spacing (r_bar=0.55) — more ergodic. Opposite of SCPN.

synthesis: Protein folding IS synchronisation (confirmed). But the coupling topology is DIFFERENT from SCPN (longer-range, more ergodic). SCPN K_nm captures a specific non-ergodic hierarchy; proteins are a different universality class.
